name: BiteBuilder Beta Stabilization and Quality Enhancements

userStories.title: Surface and classify CLI and web errors
userStories.description: As a solo filmmaker, I want clear, actionable errors for input and runtime failures so I can recover quickly without restarting from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: Return structured, user-facing errors for missing or invalid transcript file, invalid XML, malformed brief, and unsupported file content
Include error type, expected input format, and next action in every validation error
Log errors with stable machine-readable error codes in both CLI and Flask logs
Return non-zero CLI exit status for hard failures and print concise remediation text
Report recoverable status when partial progress exists (for example XML parsed but selection failed)

userStories.title: Validate transcript timecodes before LLM calls
userStories.description: As a filmmaker, I want transcript timecodes checked and normalized before generation so output stays aligned and sequence generation is safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate all transcript timecode formats against the expected schema
Reject invalid values with line-level and context references
Validate start/end ordering with explicit handling for zero-length or reversed ranges
Detect overlap or impossible transitions and reject or normalize according to policy
Preserve round-trip consistency across timecode string, frame index, and seconds in deterministic fixtures
Fail deterministically on invalid input and produce no partial XML output

userStories.title: Improve prompt quality and schema constraints
userStories.description: As an editor, I want improved prompt guardrails so selected bites are timely, relevant, and schema-compliant.
acceptanceCriteria: Define output schema constraints in the prompt
Include valid and invalid example outputs for LLM guidance
Require selected segments to align exactly to transcript timecodes
Require confidence or equivalent quality metadata for each selected bite
Document and configure deterministic seed/temperature settings for repeatability
Include fallback policy for no valid candidate results using an explicit no-op structure

userStories.title: Add LLM response validation and one-shot recovery
userStories.description: As a solo filmmaker, I want generated LLM output validated before write so broken XML is prevented and failures are recoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate LLM JSON shape before downstream XML generation
Validate each selected segment against transcript bounds and segment definitions
Return a specific parse or validation error and skip sequence writing when validation fails
Perform one automatic bounded recovery retry using a correction prompt
Show retry status and validation failure reason in the UI

userStories.title: Improve web UI workflow status and recoverability
userStories.description: As a user in the Flask UI, I want clearer step progression and recoverable interactions so I can continue editing without re-uploading input.
acceptanceCriteria: Display step markers for Upload, Validate, Preview/Confirm, Generate, and Download
Show inline input errors and panel-level messaging with Fix inputs and Try again actions
Expose running operation snapshots for received, validating, prompting, generating, and finalizing states
Preserve user input values across recoverable failures
Allow resume at each step without re-uploading transcript/XML content

userStories.title: Add fixture-backed deterministic CLI and web integration tests
userStories.description: As an engineering owner, I want end-to-end integration tests for successful and failing flows so regressions are detected before release.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CLI fixture test for transcript + XML + brief producing expected deterministic output shape
Add UI orchestration tests for success and validation-failure paths using mocked LLM responses
Add tests for malformed transcript and invalid XML fixtures
Add tests for timecode edge cases with expected structured error payloads
Ensure tests run without local Ollama by mocking model responses

userStories.title: Improve output determinism and metadata traceability
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stable outputs and trace metadata so repeated runs are debuggable and reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: Enforce deterministic ordering for segment candidate selection
Include metadata block in generated sequence XML with source hashes and input versions
Expose run metadata in CLI and UI outputs including timestamp, input descriptors, parser/validator versions, and model id
Guarantee identical output filename and content for same fixture inputs and same mock model output
